newsSince: Constructive June momentum: on/around June 12 multiple banks (Wells Fargo, Barclays, Mizuho to $500, BofA) raised targets on agentic-AI CPU demand; AGI CPU (Arm's first in-house chip) order book exceeded $2B across FY27/28 with Oracle, ByteDance, Meta, OpenAI and Supermicro server wins. Offsetting overhang: an FTC antitrust probe into Arm's licensing practices (disclosed mid-May) remains open. Stock +13.9% since June 9.

sources.label: ARM server deals / PT hikes (StocksToTrade, Jun 12)
FTC antitrust probe into Arm (Tom's Hardware)

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node-bottleneck lens: ARM's AGI-CPU order book >$2B and server wins improve fundamentals, but its directness to the true HBM/advanced-packaging/CPO bottleneck is unchanged. Modest positive; stays mid-pack vs. more direct picks-and-shovels names on this page.
verdicts.bull: Own-silicon AGI CPU adds a high-margin revenue leg on top of pervasive datacenter IP royalties.
verdicts.neutral: Bottleneck exposure is indirect; richly valued after a 222% YTD re-rating.
verdicts.bear: FTC antitrust probe and extreme valuation cap asymmetry; not a node-bottleneck pure play.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: AGI CPU order-book growth and hyperscaler/server design wins.
verdicts.primaryRisk: FTC antitrust investigation into licensing practices.

verdicts.rankRationale: CPO lens: ARM has essentially no co-packaged-optics exposure; AGI-CPU/server news is bottom-of-stack compute IP, not optical interconnect. Nothing changes its remote placement on this list. Hold near the tail.
verdicts.bull: Ubiquitous CPU IP sits adjacent to every optically-interconnected AI rack it never builds.
verdicts.neutral: No direct CPO content; ranking is correctly remote on this lens.
verdicts.bear: Zero co-packaged-optics linkage plus very high valuation; FTC overhang.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Halo from AI datacenter buildout that consumes CPO.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Valuation and FTC probe with no CPO offset.

verdicts.rankRationale: Under-owned-bottleneck master list: prior June 9 'up' already captured the asymmetry restored by the flow-driven washout. June PT hikes and >$2B AGI-CPU bookings confirm execution, but the name is now well-followed and re-rated, limiting hidden alpha. Small positive; hold.
verdicts.bull: Record FY26 plus a scaling own-chip business; licensing + silicon dual engine on AI server demand.
verdicts.neutral: Far from under-owned now; 222% YTD leaves little residual hidden alpha for this page.
verdicts.bear: FTC antitrust probe and stretched multiple; broad-alpha edge largely arbitraged away.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Pricing/execution on AI-server CPU IP and AGI-CPU ramp.
verdicts.primaryRisk: FTC antitrust action and valuation de-rating.
